content:
CHURN REPORT
Customer: Redstone Energy
Customer ID: C007
Contract ID: K008
Account Executive: Sarah Chen
Contract End Date: August 31, 2024
Report Date: September 5, 2024

REASON FOR CHURN
Redstone Energy did not renew their DataSync Pro Enterprise contract following a leadership change in their IT department. In April 2024, Redstone appointed a new CTO, Marcus Webb, who conducted a full audit of the company's SaaS stack shortly after joining.

CONTRIBUTING FACTORS
Marcus Webb came from an organization that had standardized on a competing platform, FieldOps Suite, and he initiated a consolidation effort to bring Redstone onto the same tooling he was familiar with. The decision was largely driven by his prior experience rather than dissatisfaction with DataSync Pro's performance.

Sarah Chen met with Webb twice in June and July 2024 to present a retention case, including a competitive pricing proposal and an offer to provide a dedicated implementation resource. Webb acknowledged the platform had performed well under the previous IT team but held firm on consolidation.

Existing champion, IT Director Paula Graves, who had originally championed the DataSync Pro purchase, was moved to a project management role during the restructuring and lost budget authority.

COMPETITIVE LOSS
Lost to FieldOps Suite. This is the first confirmed loss to FieldOps Suite in the Southwest region.

LESSONS LEARNED
Executive-level relationships need to be built earlier and broader. Relying on a single champion created vulnerability when that champion was moved. Recommend tracking C-suite changes in active accounts as an early warning signal.

FINAL ARR LOST: $49,500
